examples: They are at risk for a condition called osteomalacia.
When osteomalacia occurs in children, the disease is called rickets.
Calcium: phospholipid containing enough calcium, which can effectively prevent the shells of osteomalacia.
Healthy levels are about 50 nanomole per litre - less than 30 nanomole per litre can cause the softening and weakening of bones, leading to rickets in children and osteomalacia in adults.
